task_completion.description: Create a Simulated Multiparameter Monitor device adapter in OpenICE Supervisor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenICE Supervisor interface with a 'Multiparameter Monitor' selected or running in the device list/adapter creation menu.

task_completion.description: Evaluate the four required clinical demonstration applications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent opening or viewing the interfaces for 'Vital Signs', 'X-ray/Ventilator Synchronization' (Xray Viewer), 'Patient ID', and 'Infusion Safety' within OpenICE Supervisor.

task_completion.description: Create and save the clinical education guide to the correct desktop path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing a file being created and saved exactly at '/home/ga/Desktop/clinical_guide.txt'.

task_completion.description: The clinical guide includes dedicated sections for all four evaluated applications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the text file must clearly show distinct sections or headers for Vital Signs, Xray Viewer, Patient ID, and Infusion Safety.

task_completion.description: Each application section in the guide addresses the four required clinical and technical criteria.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text within the guide must describe: (1) the clinical problem/patient safety scenario, (2) required device data/connections, (3) use in daily clinical practice, and (4) how it demonstrates OpenICE interoperability for patient safety.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenICE Supervisor GUI rather than just writing the text file from memory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenICE Supervisor application open and being actively navigated by the agent.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened the specific clinical apps to evaluate them before or during the writing process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of the specific application windows (Vital Signs, X-ray, Patient ID, Infusion Safety) in the screenshots, proving the agent didn't hallucinate the guide contents.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor or standard commands to write the guide, rather than copying a pre-made script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor (like gedit, nano, or mousepad) open with the agent typing or formatting the clinical guide.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a 'Multiparameter Monitor' simulated device in OpenICE Supervisor. The four required applications to evaluate are: Vital Signs, X-ray/Ventilator Synchronization (Xray Viewer), Patient ID, and Infusion Safety (PCA infusion safety). The final output must be a text file at /home/ga/Desktop/clinical_guide.txt containing specific clinical and technical details for each of the four apps. Pass threshold is 65 points.
